provenance.source_corpus: Drivers of success in 53 cutover bogs restored by a moss layer transfer technique

nodes.name: effective blocking of the former secondary drainage network

source_spans.text: successful restoration, deﬁned by the dominance of a Sphagnum carpet (54% of all plots at the most recent observation), was mainly associated with effective blocking of the former secondary drainage network within the restored sector

successful restoration, deﬁned by the dominance of a Sphagnum carpet (54% of all plots at the most recent observation), was mainly associated with effective blocking of the former secondary drainage network within the restored sector
plots dominated by bare peat (24% of all plots) occurred more often if a hot summer followed restoration works
plots dominated by bare peat (24% of all plots) occurred more often if a hot summer followed restoration works
restoring in spring increased the likelihood of initiating an alternative successional trajectory characterised by dominance of the pioneer moss Polytrichum strictum

nodes.name: dominance of the pioneer moss Polytrichum strictum

source_spans.text: restoring in spring increased the likelihood of initiating an alternative successional trajectory characterised by dominance of the pioneer moss Polytrichum strictum

Higher donor site ratios (i.e., larger quantities of plant material introduced to the restored sector), within the range 1:5 to 1:15, were more commonly associated with better vegetation establishment

Higher donor site ratios (i.e., larger quantities of plant material introduced to the restored sector), within the range 1:5 to 1:15, were more commonly associated with better vegetation establishment

nodes.name: Higher spring and, especially, summer temperatures

source_spans.text: Higher spring and, especially, summer temperatures... were most closely associated with Bare peat-dominated restoration status
Higher spring and, especially, summer temperatures... were most closely associated with Bare peat-dominated restoration status

nodes.name: Higher precipitation in autumn and summer

source_spans.text: Higher precipitation in autumn and summer... seemed to favour the Sphagnum-dominated over the Polytrichum-dominated restoration outcome.
Higher precipitation in autumn and summer... seemed to favour the Sphagnum-dominated over the Polytrichum-dominated restoration outcome.

nodes.name: more complete blockage of secondary ditches

source_spans.text: more complete blockage of secondary ditches greatly favoured Sphagnum over P. strictum
more complete blockage of secondary ditches greatly favoured Sphagnum over P. strictum

nodes.name: longer delays between abandonment of peat extraction units and restoration

source_spans.text: links longer delays between abandonment of peat extraction units and restoration with more successful establishment of Sphagnum
links longer delays between abandonment of peat extraction units and restoration with more successful establishment of Sphagnum
larger sectors were more strongly associated with Sphagnum-dominated restoration than were smaller ones
larger sectors were more strongly associated with Sphagnum-dominated restoration than were smaller ones
delayed application of phosphorus also contributed to averting restoration failure whilst favouring P. strictum development
delayed application of phosphorus also contributed to averting restoration failure whilst favouring P. strictum development
the number of years after restoration explained 4.7% of the variability... Plots generally moved rightwards... from Bare peat-dominated into either of the other two categories.
the number of Sphagnum-dominated plots in the last year of survey was 134
